At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Hreflang Components model · Theory Hreflang Components, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 6530 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile visible purpose, canonicals, index directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and non-claims; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate codes, self links, reciprocity, canonical compatibility, reachability, and connected language alternatives.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Hreflang Components model · Theory Hreflang Components, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Syntactically valid hreflang proves localization accuracy.
